title: RA 6713, the 'Bible' of public officials
primary_topics: RA 6713 (Ethical Standards Law) as 'Bible' of public officials
anti-corruption legal arsenal alongside the Constitution, RA 3019, and RA 1379
norms, prohibited acts, SALN obligations, and graduated penalties
sub_topics: RA 6713 (Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees), approved Feb. 20, 1989
co-sponsors Jovito R. Salonga and Rene A. V. Saguisag
scope: elective and appointive, permanent or temporary, career or non-career, military and police, compensated or not
norms and duties: public interest over personal
justness and sincerity
no undue favors to relatives (except strictly confidential / co-terminus staff)
no party discrimination
prompt courteous adequate service
transparency
modest lives
duty to respond to public communications within 15 working days
process documents with no more than three official signatures where practicable
make documents accessible during reasonable working hours

prohibited acts: direct/indirect financial interest in office transactions, owning/managing regulated enterprises, private practice without authority, recommending to private enterprises with pending business
one-year cooling-off period after separation
SALN and disclosure filings (within 30 days of assumption, every April 30, within 30 days of separation)
general violations: fine â‰¤ six months' salary, suspension â‰¤ 1 year, or removal
criminal conviction for prohibited acts / SALN failure: imprisonment â‰¤ 5 years, fine â‰¤ P5,000, and/or disqualification
OMB administrative proceedings as sufficient cause for removal even without criminal prosecution
private conspirators tried jointly with public officials and subject to the same penalties

signature_phrases.phrase: stupefying billion-peso pork barrel, trillion-peso legislative insertions and other mind-bending scams
live modest lives appropriate to their positions and income
render public duties and use public funds

notable_anecdotes.summary: CJ's authorial framing: RA 6713 was 'sponsored by my guru, the late Senate President Jovito R. Salonga, the late Senator Rene A. V. Saguisag and others' â€” and approved Feb. 20, 1989

Topical anchor: 'stupefying billion-peso pork barrel, trillion-peso legislative insertions and other mind-bending scams' â€” the current scandal cycle that the law could have prevented through strict enforcement
The 'modest lives' clause cited as 'very importantly' â€” a norm directly implicating the contemporary lifestyle-check controversies CJ has tracked across multiple columns
OMB and Civil Service Commission named as the primary enforcement agents

Joint-liability doctrine: private individuals who conspire with public officials are tried jointly and subject to the same penalties â€” a doctrinal pointer toward the flood-control-scam contractor liability frame
